name: Code Screenshot MCP
what_it_does: MCP server that generates beautiful code screenshots from source code. Enables AI agents to create visually styled code images (similar to Carbon.sh) with syntax highlighting, custom themes, and formatting — useful for documentation, social sharing, and presentations.
use_cases: Generating styled code screenshots for documentation from content creation agents
Creating shareable code images with syntax highlighting for social content agents
Producing professional code visuals for presentations from documentation agents
Automating code image generation in content pipelines
not_for: Rendering code for execution or analysis (visual output only)
Real-time code previews in IDEs (better IDE plugins exist)
Large code files — screenshot generation works best for focused snippets
best_when: A content creator, developer advocate, or documentation writer wants AI agents to automatically generate beautiful code screenshots for blogs, docs, or social media.
avoid_when: You need actual code execution, analysis, or syntax checking. Code screenshots are purely visual output.

auth.notes: No authentication — local image generation tool. May call external screenshot services.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on rendering backend (local or external service)

known_agent_gotchas: Output is an image file — agent must handle binary output or image file paths
Theme and styling options should be configured upfront — verify supported options
External rendering service calls (if used) add latency and external dependency
Code sent to external screenshot services should not include sensitive data
agent_readiness.security_notes: Code snippets may be sent to external rendering services — avoid including secrets or sensitive code. Local rendering option preferred for sensitive codebases.

security_score_components.security_notes: Code may be transmitted to external screenshot services. Avoid sensitive code/secrets in input.
